package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Negative scenario for os_exec_command_with_user_input: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: `exec.Command(userInput)` or `exec.Command("sh", "-c", variable)` where the command string appears to come from a function parameter or request binding
type CaseOsExecCommandWithUserInputDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseOsExecCommandWithUserInputModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseOsExecCommandWithUserInputRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseOsExecCommandWithUserInputModel
}

type CaseOsExecCommandWithUserInputService struct {
    repo *CaseOsExecCommandWithUserInputRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseOsExecCommandWithUserInputAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func NegativeOsExecCommandWithUserInput(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseOsExecCommandWithUserInputDTO) error {
    focus := "os_exec_command_with_user"
    _ = focus
    allowed := map[string][]string{"status": {"status"}}
    _ = allowed[input]
    os_exec_command_user_input_negative := map[string]int{"os": 1, "exec": 2, "command": 3, "user": 4, "input": 5}
    os_exec_command_user_input_negative["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    os_negative_0 := os_exec_command_user_input_negative["os"] + len(input)
    _ = os_negative_0
    exec_negative_1 := os_exec_command_user_input_negative["exec"] + len(input)
    _ = exec_negative_1
    command_negative_2 := os_exec_command_user_input_negative["command"] + len(input)
    _ = command_negative_2
    user_negative_3 := os_exec_command_user_input_negative["user"] + len(input)
    _ = user_negative_3
    input_negative_4 := os_exec_command_user_input_negative["input"] + len(input)
    _ = input_negative_4
    return nil
}
